recap: Portugal survived a thrilling encounter against Croatia in Toronto, with Gonçalo Ramos heading a stoppage-time winner after Cristiano Ronaldo had equalized from the penalty spot following a VAR-reviewed foul on Renato Veiga. The match saw four goals ruled out for offside, including a stunning Ronaldo finish and a last-gasp Josko Gvardiol effort, while Croatia dominated periods after the interval through Mateo Kovacic's near-misses and Ivan Perisic's early second-half opener. Portugal's first-half dominance was undone by Croatia's improved second-half display, but Ramos's dramatic intervention in added time secured a 2-1 victory in a contest defined by VAR interventions and fine margins.
